safety_signals.visual_form: Empty foreground space with solid platforms

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level environment fully rendered with obstacles and enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrols and spawns active; platforming hazards present

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HUD indicator shows low/empty resource meters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited player action capability (jetpack unusable or weapons unavailable)
No new enemy spawns; progression enabled

environmental_storytelling.example: NES platformer converts The Rocketeer film into level-based combat/traversal narrative
Fuel and ammo meters visually show player vulnerability progression
Enemy distribution reflects danger zones aligned with movie plot progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Jetpack active; sprite shows upward propulsion animation
Jetpack disabled; falls subject to gravity; grounded movement only
Sprite animation cycle indicates active threat status

notes: Available source material (TCRF wiki stub) provides minimal visual communication data. Game mechanics inferred from platformer genre conventions and NES technical constraints. Movie license context suggests enemies/environments derive from 1991 film source material. Debug features present (level select, health/fuel restoration) indicate developer intent for state manipulation, but visual manifestations of these states not documented in source.
